page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the N1 family, configured with 32 vCPUs and 208 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, this instance features 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated, supported by a 45 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression, consistently falls into the weak category. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, achieve average tier results. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also register in the average tier, with uncached read bandwidth reaching 95.75 GB/sec.

From a workload perspective, the server is suited for memory-intensive applications that benefit from a 6.5 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio but do not require high single-threaded performance. Average performance in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving makes it viable for relational databases, caching layers, and web serving. It also supports light machine learning tasks, demonstrating average performance in LLM inference for small and medium models, though it performs poorly on larger models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-allocated server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 208 GB of RAM. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 16 physical cores, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core performance but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy legacy workloads, database operations, and caching layers that do not demand high single-threaded clock speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs and 208 GB RAM. It features average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, making it suitable for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 208 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core server featuring high memory density for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 208 GB of system memory
Intel Xeon 2.3 GHz processor with 16 physical cores
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for databases, caching layers, and web serving
